对于不耐烦，有一个GitHub存储库，用于此处文档的MapTrace程序，它可以齐全的输入和Quick Perusal。
  我的月光不时作为图形设计师，我在这个角色中承担的不常见任务之一是帮助人们清理演示和出版物的地理地图。最近的一个项目涉及说明基于来自SIL International的数据的扬声器的位置。以下是由此产生的地图作为PNG文件（从原始大小下载8x）：
  实现这一目标的正确方法将触发QGIS或其他实际的GIS软件，并创建Shapefilescorr对应的地理区域的aset。但由于我的“客户”1在一段时间紧缩，我们在Photoshop中追踪了追踪照片和用ThePaint桶工具用手填充地区。是的，这是一个粗略的解决方案，但它按时获得了公开，而客户目前正在研究Aproper GIS表示。 2
 与此同时，客户端希望生成一些替代的ColorSchemes以突出特定地区进行另一个演示（在相当快速的时间表上onceagain），并且我认为在插画师而不是Photoshop中完成了一个非常糟糕的任务，因为Vecortogrom的图形更容易当它来到扁平区域的颜色时使用。
 因此，我编写了一个程序来获取原始地图并将其转换为可扩展的向量图形（SVG）文件，该文件可以轻松导入Illustrator。它最终练习非常好 - 这是最终输出（点击放大）：
  让我们看看两张图像的一些细节。对于每个人，左侧来自原始的快速和脏水平输出，右侧来自栅格化的PRES输出的程序。您可以使用涂料桶工具看到许多微小的流苏术，以洪水填充地区在本土中;它们完全在矢量输出中消失了。
    SVG文件也提供了很多压缩：原来的PNG重量约为2.5米（当Pngquant压缩时减少到1.0米）。相比之下，输出SVG为105K，但仅在GZIP压缩时为38K。 
所以它不仅看起来很多，最终输出大约比原来小25倍！ （嗯，让我想起我的NoteShrink项目......）
   首先，如果它可用，则始终更好地使用OricemedGeEREFERENCED数据。其次，这是来自GIS角度的不完整性系统，因为它不会产生GeoreFerencedOutput。如果您的输入数据是地理的，则运行程序时将丢失所有此类信息。
 最后，请不要使用此软件来规避现有地图的版权所有限制！目的版权目的，您应该考虑MapTrace的输出是原始输入的衍生作品。因此，只有发布MapTrace输出，如果您被许可以重现原始输入。
  已经存在自动将栅格（位图）图像转换为向量格式，包括Adobe Illustrator的实时跟踪/图像Tracetools或像Peter Selinger的Potrace等独立程序，它在Inkscape中提供了可比的功能。
 然而，所有这些工具都分享了MapMaking的常见缺点 - 给定了一个简单的光栅线描的LikeThis Heart：
  ......他们实际上生产了两个轮廓，一个用于内部，一个外面的一个。您可以通过重新着色Potrace的输出来演示这一点，以便冲击每个轮廓：
  我们愿意的是用单个轮廓替换概述，该轮廓可以划推任何所需的宽度。这是我MapTrace工具的输出： 
实际上，MapTrace还产生了围绕的两种轮廓（对应于光线和黑暗的Regionsabove的边界）;然而，它们完全重叠，允许用户将边界划衡到他们所允许的任何宽度。
 从3D建模社区借用，我将指的是完全重叠的轮廓作为水密地图，因为每个地图区域的边界（即，心脏上面的内部和外部）与邻居完全一致，留下没有差距。生产防水地图是我在承担这个项目的主要技术性技能。
  当我在组织写这篇文章时读完时，我跑过几个相关的努力：
  我相信这是一个不完整的名单，那里可能有很多有趣的相关工作。
  这是MapTrace如何从栅格输入到输出SVG的逐步细分。
  首先，阈值将原始灰度或RGB图像转换为1-BPP BooleAnmask，指示找到概述的1-BPP BooleAnMask。得到的掩模是非举行的红色，绿色或蓝色通道的最大值超过漏洞阈值，或者无论alpha通道低于某种方式阈值（例如，我们假设透明区域不是MapOutlines）。
   由阈值操作产生的掩模无论何处都是零，那么在其他地方均为零，其他地方： 
接下来，我们可以选择将形态运算符作为侵蚀，扩张，打开或关闭到掩模图像。 TIMCAN对大坝中的小空隙很有用，或者加入附近的邻近的轮廓，由非常小的轮廓分开。我们将省略yin-yang示例的步骤，但请参阅github存储库中的readme.md。（查找-f命令行选项）。
  现在我们拍摄上面计算的掩码，并执行连接组件标签，以识别图像中的唯一非轮廓区域。在乌黄阳之阳中，有五个地区，每个地区分配了一个随机的颜色（轮廓本身是留下白色）：
    我们现在获得掩模图像的欧几里德距离变换（EDT）。 EDT将每个外部kex的距离计算为最接近的非外形像素。以下是一种可视化（在轮廓内进一步嵌入更亮的像素）：
  对于我们的EDT的应用，我们不关心STILESTHEM我们实际上对TheClosest非概述像素的标签非常感兴趣。当分配每个大纲像素的Thislabel时，非轮廓区域的增长，我们得到了一个没有翻磁的地图：
  现在是时候停止使用栅格并开始代表图像作为一组矢量轮廓。
  接下来，我们沿着每个标记区域的概要追踪（也是图像的边界）以创建一组节点和边缘。沿着像素Borderingtwo不同区域的边界的每个步骤是一系列步骤，并且节点是它们的交叉点（沿着闭合边缘的奥萨里亚点）。
 下面的SVG图显示了节点作为黑圆圈和边缘截图，其颜色表示其边界的标记区域： 
这种追踪操作是该程序的最慢的部分，因为它都与Python中的天真循环完成。它可以通过并将其倾向于灰色来加速这一操作来加速这一操作，但我可能更喜欢将整个程序移植到编译的唐扬州。
  接下来，我们可以选择合并附近的节点，然后应用Ramer-Douglas-Peucker算法，简化每个边缘，具有用户提供的公差。这对于消除从像素对齐的路径中断的小方形锯齿时使用。结果看起来像这样：
   最后，我们生成输出SVG。对于每个标记的区域，我们循环边界边界并将其连接到一个或疯狂的边缘。 3我们可以为每个区域分配随机颜色（正如我们在此示例中所做的sofar），从原始图像中提取颜色，或者甚至从相同大小和形状的另一个图像均衡它们。
 作为命令行选项，我们可以告诉该计划用一个突出线中描绘地区（通常是背景或海洋），往往看起来非常好。这是最终输出SVG Forour Yin-yang符号示例：
  这是从GitHub存储库中拉出的另一个示例。 WikelIpput Image是2012年宾夕法尼亚州的2012年选举地图，从维基百科拉动：
     连接组件标签但在区域生长之前，我们可以删除非常小的地区，其区域低于Certainthreshold。这对于摆脱单像素的漏洞是很好的，但是，Butcan也可用于从地图上删除文本，如美国宾夕法尼亚州的普斯ylvania县的人口普查，也取自维基百科：
  以下是删除任何区域位于1000像素以下的封闭区域后产生的SVG文件： 
Potrace及其商业类似物的一个区域有效地映射映射在他们识别和输出路径的能力。 相比之下，我的程序输出的每个矢量都是ispolygonal的。 缩小后，它们看起来很好，但在洛地卢切得不好看。 让我们称之为未来的工作。  此外，由于它是在Python中编写的，MapTrace在编译语言中的优化实现将较慢。 当我会到处时，我一直试图尝试一下，我一直有意思一会儿，也许我会在一定节目中伸展一下......  无论如何，这是关于它的。 随意跟进Twitterwith您可能有任何问题或评论！  或者至少支付本科委员会的RA来处理适当的GIS解决方案。 ↩  注意，具有孔的区域必须由多于一个轮廓形成。 ↩  为读者锻炼：为什么沿着国家边界的小线被删除？ ↩
note_en:
For the impatient, there’s a github repository for the maptrace program documented here, and it comes complete with example inputs and outputsfor quick perusal.
  From time to time, I moonlight as a graphic designerand one of the infrequent tasks I take on in this role is helpingpeople clean up geographic maps for presentation and publication. One recent project involved illustrating the locations of speakers ofvarious languages based on data from SIL International. Here’s the resulting map as a PNG file (downsampled 8X from the original size):
  The  right way to accomplish this would have been to fire up QGIS or other actual  GIS software, and create aset of  shapefilescorresponding to the geographic regions of interest. But since my“client”  1 was under a time crunch, we settled on tracing over anexisting map in Photoshop and filling in regions by hand with thePaint Bucket tool. Yes, this was a gross solution but it got thepublication out on time, and the client is currently working on aproper GIS representation.  2
 In the meantime, the client wanted to generate a few alternative colorschemes to highlight particular regions for another presentation (onceagain on a fairly rapid timetable) and I thought it would be a mucheasier task to accomplish in Illustrator rather than Photoshop, sincevector graphics are much easier to work with when it comes toselecting colors for flat regions.
 So I wrote a program to take the original map and convert it to a scalable vector graphics (SVG)file that can be easily imported into Illustrator. It ended up workingout really nicely – here’s the final output (click to enlarge):
  Let’s take a look at a few details from the two images. For each ofthese, the left-hand side comes from the original quick-and-dirtyPhotoshop output, and the right-hand side comes from rasterizing theSVG output of the program. You can see lots of tiny fringing artifactsfrom using the Paint Bucket tool to flood-fill regions in theoriginal; they are completely gone in the vector output.
    The SVG file affords a good deal of compression, too: the original PNG weighs inat about 2.5M (reduced to 1.0M when lossily compressed by  pngquant). In contrast, the output SVG is105K but just 38K when gzip-compressed.
 So not only does it look a lot nicer, the final output is about 25X smaller than the original!  (Hmm, reminds me of  my noteshrink project…)
   First of all, it’s always better to use originalgeoreferenced data if it’s available. Second, this is an incompletesystem from a GIS standpoint because it doesn’t produce georeferencedoutput. If your input data is georeferenced, you will lose all of thisinformation when the program is run.
 Finally, please  do not use thissoftware to circumvent copyright restrictions on existing maps! Forthe purposes of copyright, you should consider the output of maptrace a derivative work of the original input. So, only publish  maptrace output if you are licensed to reproduce the original input.
  There already exist tools to automatically convert raster (bitmap)images to vector formats, including Adobe Illustrator’s Live Trace/ Image Tracetools or standalone programs like Peter Selinger’s   potrace, which provides the  comparable functionality in Inkscape.
 However, all of these tools share a common shortcoming from theperspective of mapmaking – given a simple raster line drawing likethis heart:
  …they actually produce  two contours, one for the inside, and one forthe outside. You can demonstrate this by re-coloring the output of potrace to stroke each contour:
  What we would prefer is to replace the outline with a single contour that can be strokedto any desired width. Here’s the output from my  maptrace tool:
  In actuality,  maptrace also produces two contours surrounding theheart (corresponding to the borders of the light and dark regionsabove); however, they overlap completely, allowing the user to strokethe border to whatever width they please.
 Borrowing from the 3D modeling community, I’m going to refer to thisproperty of completely overlapping outlines as a  watertight map,since the boundary of each map region (i.e. the interior and exteriorof the heart above) is completely coincident with its neighbor,leaving no gaps. Producing watertight maps was my main technicalobjective in undertaking this project.
  As I was reading up when getting organized to write this post, I ran acrossa couple of related efforts:
  I’m sure this is an incomplete list, and there’s probably lots more interesting related work out there.
  Here’s the step-by-step breakdown of how  maptrace goes from a raster input to an output SVG.
  First, the input image is thresholdedto convert the original grayscale or RGB image into a 1-bpp booleanmask indicating where outlines are found. The resulting mask is nonzerowherever the  maximum of the red, green, or blue channels exceeds abrightness threshold, OR wherever the alpha channel falls below someopacity threshold (e.g., we assume transparent areas are not mapoutlines).
   The mask produced by the thresholding operation is zero wherever theyin-yang outline falls, and one everywhere else:
  Next, we can optionally apply morphological operatorssuch as erosion, dilation, opening, or closing to the mask image. Thiscan be useful to dam small gaps in the outlines or join nearbyregions separated by very small bits of outline. We’re going to omitthis step for the yin-yang example, but see the README.md in the github repositoryfor examples (look for the  -f command-line option).
  Now we take the mask computed above and perform connected-component labelingto identify the unique non-outline regions within the image. In ouryin-yang example, there are five regions, each assigned a random colorhere (the outline itself is left white):
    We now obtain the Euclidean Distance Transform (EDT)of the mask image. The EDT computes the distance from every outlinepixel to the closest non-outline pixel. Here is a visualization(brighter pixels are embedded further inside the outline):
  For our application of the EDT, we don’t care about the distancesthemselves; we are actually far more interested in the label of theclosest non-outline pixel. When each outline pixel is assigned thislabel, the non-outline regions grow, and we obtain a map with no gapsbetween them at all:
  Now it’s time to stop working with rasters and begin representing the image as a set of vector outlines.
  Next, we trace along the outline of each labeled region (as well asthe borders of the image) to create a set of nodes and edges. Eachedge is a sequence of steps along the boundaries of pixels borderingtwo distinct regions, and the nodes are their intersection points (orarbitrary points along closed edges).
 The SVG figure below shows the nodes as black circles and the edges asdotted lines whose colors indicate the labeled regions they border:
  This tracing operation is by far the slowest partof the program, since it’s all done with naïve loops in Python. It’stheoretically possible to speed this operation up by parallelizing it orcalling out to   Cython but I’d probably rather portthe entire program to a compiled langauge eventually instead.
  Next, we can optionally merge nearby nodes and then apply the Ramer-Douglas-Peucker algorithmto simplify each edge, with user-supplied tolerances. This isuseful for eliminating the little square jaggies that result fromfollowing pixel-aligned paths. The results look like this:
   Finally, we generate the output SVG. For each labeled region, we loopover the edges that border it and concatenate them into one or morecontours.  3 We can assign random colors per region (as we have done sofar in this example), pull colors in from the original image, or evenassign them from another image of the same size and shape.
 As a command-line option, we can tell the program to stroke thelargest region (usually the background or ocean) with a largeroutline, which often looks quite nice. Here’s the final output SVG forour yin-yang symbol example:
  Here’s another example pulled from the github repository. The Originalinput image is the 2012 electoral map for Pennsylvania, pulled from Wikipedia:
     After connected component labeling but before region growing, we canoptionally delete very small regions whose areas fall below a certainthreshold. This is nice for getting rid of single-pixel blips, butcan also be used to remove text from maps, as in this US Census Bureaumap of Pennsylvania counties, also taken from Wikipedia:
  Here’s the resulting SVG file after deleting any enclosed region whose area is under 1000 pixels:
   One area where  potrace and its commercial analogues significantlyoutperform  maptrace is in their ability to recognize and outputcurved paths. In contrast, every vector output by my program ispolygonal. They look fine when zoomed out, but don’t look great at lowresolution. Let’s call that future work.
 Additionally, since it’s written in Python,  maptrace is much slowerthan an optimized implementation in a compiled language would be. Notsure when I’ll get around to it, but I’ve been meaning to try out Rust for a while, maybe I’ll take a crack atporting it someday…
 Anyways, that’s about it for now. Feel free to follow up on twitterwith any questions or comments you might have!
 Or at least, paying an undergrad RA to work on a proper GIS solution.  ↩
  Note that regions with holes will necessarily be formed by more than one contour.  ↩
  Exercise for the reader: why do the little lines along the border of the state get deleted?  ↩
title: 来自光栅图像的水密矢量地图
title_en: Watertight vector maps from raster images

一个懒惰的程序员喜欢编程，但讨厌工作，所以他们尽可能少地工作。  一个懒惰的程序员写了很多测试，所以QA Junks不会浪费时间。  一个懒惰的程序员是代表团的主人。 在他们委托任务后，他们立即忘记了它。  懒惰的程序员没有编辑长文件，他们教导正则表达式如何在他们的位置编辑文件。  懒人编程器不部署生产，他们指示詹金斯这样做。 因此，一个懒惰的程序员不怕星期五下午部署。  一个懒惰的程序员尽可能少地前往办公室，可能当其他非懒人不在周围时，所以他们不会浪费时间。  一个懒惰的程序员知道他们的交易的最佳工具，以便他们尽可能重复使用其他人员工作。 
一个懒惰的程序员很容易，因为争论愚蠢的事情是，嗯，愚蠢和累人。  一个懒惰的程序员是超级效率，在几个小时内完成了一整天给别人，这样他们就可以花在剩下的时间里挥之不去，在短鲑鱼上徘徊。  一个懒惰的程序员在代码中启动几个小时，试图弄清楚尽可能少的代码。  懒惰的程序员利用现有的工具和服务，因此他们可以先发布。  一个懒惰的程序员使用托管服务提供基本的UI模板，然后他们说它是野蛮主义。  在Saasform，我们是懒惰的，我们支持懒惰的程序员，以快速提供优秀的代码，并且很少努力。
note_en:
A lazy programmer loves programming, but hates working, so they work as little as possible.
  A lazy programmer writes a lot of tests, so QA junks do not waste their time.
  A lazy programmer is a master of delegation. After they delegated a task, they immeditaly forget about it.
 A lazy programmer do not edit long files, they teach regular expressions how to edit the file in their place.
 A lazy programmer do not deploy in production, they instruct Jenkins to do that. Therefore a lazy programmer is not afraid of deploying on Friday afternoon.
 A lazy programmer goes to the office as little as possible, possibly when other non-lazy people are not around, so they don’t waste their time.
 A lazy programmaer knows the best tools of their trade, so that they can reuse other people work as much as possible.
 A lazy programmer is easy going, because arguing about silly things is, well, silly and tiring.
 A lazy programmer is super efficient, does in a couple of hours what would take a whole day to others, so that they can spend the rest of they lingering on the couch feasting on Netflix.
 A lazy programmer startes at the code for hours, trying to figure out the way to write as little code as possible.
 A lazy programmer makes use of existing tools and services, so they can release earlier.
 A lazy programmer uses the basic UI template the hosting service provides them and then they say it’s  brutalism.
 At  Saasform we are lazy and we support lazy programmers to deliver great code fast and with little effort.
title: 你也是懒惰的程序员吗？
title_en: Are you a lazy programmer too?

在我们的Hackspace中，我们在上个月开设了纺织房间。我们希望它具有很高的希望，作为COSPLAYERS和LAPERS的焦点以及可穿戴电子产品和更传统的纺织用户的制造商。把它涉及几个月的努力工作，带来一个半遗弃和以前洪水的房间，这是我们当地的学校权威的行走安全，这是一个光明和热情的空间，但令人惊讶的数量工作也必须赢得我们社区的心灵和思想。
 简单地说，纺织品并未被视为非常酷，在黑客空间术语中。你知道，女人的东西。你的母亲做到了，甚至可能是如果你有点年轻，你的祖母。它只是没有激光切割或3D打印的那样，因此，我们为什么这是一个大部分制作的东西，在空间内的资源方面必须打击它的角落。
 然而，不久前我当我在社交之夜带来一个磨损的牛仔裤并拖出了我们的执事缝纫机来解决它们时，我常常通过我在所做的事情上传递的同伴。 “你可以修理牛仔裤吗？”他们被要求问道。出于某种原因，这一前景没有发生，我在服装转世的新景观中，在我们的新设施之前太长的程度，我可能会在牛津黑客的心爱的前裤子上给一个关于主体的研讨会亚德森获得了新生活的机会。
   关于这一似乎在制造商和纺织品之间的奇怪事物之一是它在两个方向上工作。正如我的哈克斯普斯的朋友在纺织品工作中努力看到价值，所以我的纺织爱好者朋友们经常无法催促他们的技术成就水平。从硬件黑客的角度考虑这一点：他们的每个项目都涉及在柔性材料中制作一个三维物体，既完全适合单独的佩戴者，并且只使用卷尺读数在2D介质中良好的佩戴者。和平方纸假设他们正在制作自己的模式。不是CAD包或在视线中渲染预览！这是1337级的令人敬畏，但他们认为这是众所周心的东西，因为他们的母亲或祖母展示了他们如何。
 现在也许你会理解为什么我们希望我们的纺织空间很高，这些是可以做出任何事情的人，只要我们能吸引他们的空间是一个资产的人。
 所以你可能会问，这是纺织主义者的描述和一个Hackspace在这个领域的工作，它对我来说是什么？坚持下去，我们会看看缝纫机，而不是你祖母的珍贵占有，而是作为原来的家用机床，也许在你知道它的工作原理后，你会看到你为什么要为一个空间腾出空间你的研讨会。
  最基本的缝纫方法涉及用手和螺纹用手进行。你最有可能以这种方式创造的缝线是后缝，你在其中靠近缝制每隔一针的方向，以锁定缝线的线，如果一个线条突破，可以停止它分开。它非常慢，需要大量的技能来实现良好的结果。即使您拥有缝纫机，除非您正在制作历史服装的超级精确娱乐，否则这是您偶尔需要做的事情，除非您正在为整个项目使用的东西。 
缝纫机不能使用单个螺纹进行止回阀，当您可能期望时，机器非常困难，虽然在每个针迹上整体地通过织物。相反，它使用两个线程来创建一个名为LockStitch的针，其中每个螺纹在其自己的侧面停留在织物的侧面，并仅通过针头制造的孔与另一个螺纹互锁。上螺纹来自机器顶部的卷轴，而下部的阀芯来自其工作表面下方的筒管。制造锁定器的作用是通过缝纫机发明的各种机构来执行，但在大多数人中，您将通过围绕线轴的旋转钩完成。该钩子拾取了通过针头推穿过织物的上螺纹的环，然后在针和上机构将上螺纹紧密穿过孔之前将其从筒管上绕过下螺纹。这产生可靠且一致的缝合，可以以非常高的速度重复。
 您可以看到这里有很多事情：机器必须以正确的速度和张力提供线程，保持面料到位，以正确的速度向前移动，并保持整个批次同步。缝纫机是复杂的野兽。
  缝纫机在平坦表面上具有针机构，悬挂在水平臂上，以提供足够的床宽度，以在针的任一侧将工作定位。绝大多数机器具有从右侧进入的水平臂，来自垂直臂，通常包含电动机和驱动机构的重要部分。在机器的极端右手侧是外侧的车轮，随着机构的操作而旋转，这可能历史上有一个手曲柄或滑轮，用于踏板或外部电动机以供电。它有助于允许操作员非常缓慢地手动前进，或者退回机制。在较旧的机器上，它将是一款大型金属滑轮，它将恢复到手工摇晃或踏板的日子里，而在较新的轮子上通常会采用塑料旋钮的形式。
 滑轮的左侧是夹持齿轮，轴或带传递到针的两个臂，向下移动到机器床下方。选择功能和调整针脚长度，筒管卷绕机和光的控制通常安装在臂的外侧，并且螺纹线轴坐在机器顶部的销上
 在水平臂和床的左侧，我们拥有机器的业务端：针和梭芯组件。我们将从顶部向下考虑这些，因为它们大致在该轴的一行中。
 在大多数机器上，您可以在隐藏机构的水平臂的末端上拆下板或盖子。如果要将其移除并查看手臂的末端，那么您将看到驱动轴的末端用曲柄驱动升高和降低针的机构。该曲柄还经营螺纹卷取杆，该螺纹卷筒通常从前壳体突出，这使得螺纹环绕其圆形，并且具有将螺纹环拉出通过每个单独的针脚上的织物拉动螺纹的功能。还将有一个螺纹张紧器，类似于一对夹紧线的垫圈，用小弹簧的力夹紧螺纹。
  垂直上臂末端的机器的整个高度将是两个条。朝向机器的前部将是用针连接到其底部的针杆，而后面将是压脚杆。该棒底部的压力脚用于将织物保持在机器的床上，并且可以在机器背面升起或降低。 
下压脚下是机器的床，下面是上面描述的梭芯和钩机构。针的两侧都是一对齿状狗，该狗在曲柄上行动，以“走路”穿过针。
 如果您在最后几段中遵循了描述，您现在可以了解简单的直线缝纫机的工作。这些是十九世纪中叶的标准，直到20世纪60年代的某个时候，当时具有其他缝线等锯齿形或双排缝线的国内机器出现。这些机器具有额外的机制，允许针头从侧向到侧面以及上下移动，首先通过可选凸轮控制的东西，而是通过微控制器更新的机器。
 一旦你有缝纫机，你可能会这样做什么？当然，与任何其他多功能工具或机器一样，答案仅限于您的想象力或您最喜欢的搜索引擎。如果这不是你的事情，如果不是你的事情，就没有理由使用，如果这不是你的话，那么如果只有他们有一个纺织品，就可以在他们的项目中找到一些纺织品的地方在媒介中工作。我偶尔的服装项目并已用于一些万圣节服装，但到目前为止，他们看到的最多使用是在修理和修改我已经拥有的东西。从修复我的日子包的磨损边缘，修补这些珍贵的人，但过去 - 当我的朋友的遮阳篷有一个撕裂时，他们的牛仔裤和拯救了一个大篷车假期，他们就像我的万用表或示波器一样重要的工具。
  如果你这么远，想要寻找一个缝纫机来恩典自己的长凳，你应该在哪里开始？鉴于这是一个超过150年的生产的产品，您可以选择大量机器。
 它值得在市场尽头开始，您可能不会指望我们先解决，并查看新机器。一台新机器可能超过二手二手，但如果你明智地购买，你应该能够获得足够的现代特征而不破坏银行。当然就像任何工具一样，可以在市场的顶端支付四或五个数字总和，如果这是您拍摄的课程，您将获得以非常高质量的机器的形式获得的。但如果这描述了你，那么你可能不需要我们的建议。对于本文的目的，我们对市场的另一端更感兴趣。
  如果您看看任何百货商店网站，您将找到一系列预算缝纫机。在价格范围的底部，其中一些人在50美元或下面的价格令人惊讶。这些通常是拥有的品牌机器，针对年轻用户，一种“我的第一台缝纫机”产品。这可能最好将这些机器与玩具更加共同，而不是祖母的信赖歌手，避免它们。你可以用一个人制作各种各样的东西，但是不要指望它是强大的，或者在用于重型工作时持续时间。
 快乐地，在玩具机上的下一步是质量制造商的基础型号。这些分享了金属部件和更昂贵的弟兄们的可靠性，但缺乏一些高级功能。它们通常有一系列缝线，但它们的机制将完全机械而不是计算机化。如果您在100美元以上的某处以备用备件，他们为寻求工作室缝纫机的人提供了绝佳的选择。 
在二手市场上，您拥有本世纪或更多的机器可供选择，有些机器可以指挥眼花价格，而其他机器几乎毫无价值。最好从最近的机器开始，并及时向后移动以捕捉甜点。
  20世纪60年代后期到20世纪90年代的大多数高级机器都有一天中的所有现代特征，如针迹范围，并且依靠制造商建造在非常高的标准。然而，它们的外表非常沉重，非常繁重，所以可以在想要的广告中常常在非常好的情况下找到，而不是大量的钱。在写作这件作品时，快速扫描发现一系列类似的机器，包括来自非常昂贵的制造商的厂家，广告为前面提到的玩具机的类似价格。汇率目前处于助焊状态，所以进行转换可能是不明智的，但是如果您不介意在其上缝制一些肩部垫的机器，则可能是25英镑到50英镑。时间。
 较旧的机器来自1850年代到20世纪60年代的一个不间断的线。他们都表现出直线拼接的单一任务，而二十世纪初，他们的设计已经发展到他们所有相当类似的机械上。在关于第一次世界大战之前的早期模型可能最好考虑在这里作为博物馆的目的，它是来自本世纪第二季度的后来应该感兴趣的人。
 早期的机器，如页面顶部的黑白图片中的一个，被自己成为美丽的物体。他们装饰丰富，并有收藏家为这款装修买它们。与收集者以其价格移动的任何领域一样，可以攀登仅适用于可用的缝纫机的买方的点才能为他们提供价格。
  我们的好消息是来自中世纪的时尚。在20世纪50年代，带有弯曲铸件的装饰机器被视为丑陋和过时，客户要求使用现代色调的清洁线路。因此，制造商拿走了几十年的机器，并将它们躺在涂上现代色彩的更多角度身体上。时尚是一件有趣的事情，因为这些沉闷的棕色和绿色缝纫机现在是不受欢迎和不受欢迎的，尽管力地与弯曲和装饰的前辈机械相同。这是一个好消息，因为它意味着他们的价值较少，持久性可能会拿出你一个接近啤酒的东西。
 这20世纪50年代和20世纪60年代早期的机器建成了极高的标准，并将缝制任何您可以投掷的东西。他们可能几乎所有其他所有的东西都会超过。您确实需要将内在的举重器召唤到一个人，而且他们不会宽松易于运输。如果你可以在没有任何花哨的缝线的情况下代表一些甜蜜的地方，如果你能够掌握一下，我们会推荐他们作为开始的好地方。
 你有没有改变纺织工作阅读这篇文章的思想，你是为了为你的祖母被遗忘的缝纫机冲刷你的父母阁楼？在一个硬件黑客的替补席上，缝纫机只是另一台机床，可能是一个你不能没有的机床。
note_en:
In our hackspace, we’ve opened a textile room in the last month. We have high hopes for it as a focal point for cosplayers and LARPers as well as the makers of wearable electronics and more traditional textile users. Putting it in has involved several months of hard work bringing a semi-derelict and previously flooded room that was once the walk-in safe for our local school authority to a point at which it is a light and welcoming space, but a surprising amount of work has also had to go into winning the hearts and minds of our community for the project.
 Putting it quite simply, textiles aren’t seen as very cool, in hackspace terms. You know,  Women’s stuff. Your mother does it, or even maybe if you are a little younger, your grandmother. It’s just not up there with laser cutting or 3D printing, and as a result those of us for whom it’s a big part of making stuff have had to fight its corner when it comes to resources within the space.
 Yet not so long ago when I brought a pair of worn-out jeans into the space on a social night and hauled out our Lervia sewing machine to fix them, I had a constant stream of fellow members passing by amazed at what I was doing. “You can  repair jeans?” they asked, incredulously. For some reason this prospect had not occurred to them, I was opening up a new vista in clothing reincarnation, to the extent that before too long in our new facility I may be giving a workshop on the subject as the beloved former trousers of Oxford Hackspace denizens gain a chance of new life.
   One of the odd things about this seeming gulf between makers and textiles is that it works in both directions. Just as my hackspace friends had struggled to see the worth in textile work, so do my textile enthusiast friends often fail to bask in their level of technical achievement. Think about this from a hardware hacker perspective: their every project involves making a three-dimensional object in a flexible material that both fits an individual wearer perfectly and looks good on them, and all this by hand in a 2D medium using only tape measure readings and squared paper assuming they’re making their own pattern. Not a CAD package or rendered preview in sight! That’s 1337 levels of awesome, yet they take it for granted as something the’ve always done, because their mother or grandmother showed them how.
 Now perhaps you’ll understand why we have high hopes of our textile space, these are people who can make  anything, just the sort of members who’d be an asset to our space if we can attract them.
 So you might be asking, that’s a description of textilists and one hackspace’s work in the field, what’s in it for me? Stick around, and we’ll take a look at the sewing machine not as your grandmother’s prized possession but as the original home machine tool, and maybe after you know something of how it works you’ll see why you should make space for one in your workshop.
  The most basic method of sewing involves doing it by hand, with a single needle and thread. The stitch you’d be most likely to create in this way is  backstitch, in which you go back against the direction of sewing every other stitch to lock the line of stitches and stop it coming apart if a thread breaks. It’s very slow, and requires a considerable amount of skill to achieve a good result. It’s something you occasionally need to do even if you own a sewing machine, but unless you are making super-accurate recreations of historical clothing it’s not something you’ll use for a whole project.
 The sewing machine can’t do a backstitch with a single thread as you might expect, it’s very difficult for a machine to pass the thread though the fabric in its entirety on each stitch. Instead it uses two threads to create a stitch called lockstitch, in which each thread stays on its own side of the fabric and interlocks with the other thread only through the holes made by the needle. The upper thread comes from a spool on the top of the machine while the lower one comes from a bobbin mounted underneath its operating surface. The action of making the lockstitch has been performed by a variety of mechanisms since the invention of the sewing machine, but in the majority of those you’ll encounter it is done through a rotating hook that surrounds the bobbin. This hook picks up a loop of the upper thread pushed through the fabric by the needle, and wraps it round the lower thread from the bobbin before the needle and upper mechanism pulls the upper thread tight back up through the hole. This produces reliable and consistent stitching that can be repeated at very high speed.
 You can see that there’s a lot going on here: the machine has to supply the thread at the right speed and tension, hold the fabric in place, move it forwards at the correct speed, and keep the whole lot in synchronisation. Sewing machines are complex beasts.
  A sewing machine has a needle mechanism over a flat surface, suspended from a horizontal arm to give enough bed width to position the work as necessary on either side of the needle. The overwhelming majority of machines have the horizontal arm coming in from the right, coming from a vertical arm that usually contains the motor and significant parts of the drive mechanism. On the extreme right hand side of the machine is a wheel on the outside that rotates as the mechanism operates, this might historically have had a hand crank or pulley for a treadle or external motor to power the sewing. It serves to allow the operator to manually advance the stitches very slowly, or to back off the mechanism. On older machines it will be a large metal pulley that harks back to the hand-cranked or treadle days, while on newer ones it will usually take the form of a plastic knob.
 To the left of the pulley are the two arms containing the gears, shafts, or belts transfer motion to the needle and down to below the bed of the machine. The controls to select functions and adjust stitch length, a bobbin winder, and a light are usually mounted on the outside of the arms, and the spool of thread sits on a pin on top of the machine
 At the far left of the horizontal arm and the bed, we have the business end of the machine: the needle, and the bobbin assembly. We’ll consider these from the top downwards, as they are all roughly in a line in that axis.
 On most machines you can remove the plate or cover on the end of the horizontal arm that conceals the mechanism. If you were to remove it and look at the end of the arm face-on you’d see the end of the drive shaft with a crank that drives the mechanism that raises and lowers the needle. This crank also operates the thread take-up lever which normally protrudes from the front casing, this has the thread looped round it and has the function of pulling the loop of thread back up through the fabric on each individual stitch. There will also be a thread tensioner, similar to a pair of washers clamping the thread with the force of a small spring.
  Vertically the entire height of the machine at the end of the arm will be two bars. Towards the front of the machine will be the needle bar with the needle attached to its bottom, while behind it will be the presser foot bar. The presser foot at the bottom of this bar serves the purpose of holding the fabric down on the bed of the machine, and can be raised or lowered with a lever on the back of the machine.
 Below the presser foot is the bed of the machine, and below that is the bobbin and hook mechanism described above. Either side of the needle are a pair of toothed dogs that act on a crank to “walk” the fabric past the needle.
 If you have followed the description in the last few paragraphs you will now have a good idea of the working of a simple straight-stitch sewing machine. These were the standard from the middle of the nineteenth century until sometime in the 1960s when domestic machines capable of other stitches such as zig-zags or dual-row stitching appeared. These machines have an extra mechanism that allows the needle to move from side to side as well as up and down, something controlled at first by selectable cams but on more recent machines by a microcontroller.
 Once you have a sewing machine, what might you do with it? Of course, like any other versatile tool or machine, the answer is limited only by your imagination or your favourite search engine. And there should be no reason for you to have to use if for making garments if that’s not your thing, there are a huge number of places a maker can find use for a bit of textile work in their projects if only they have the means to work in the medium. I make the occasional garment project and have been used for a few Halloween costumes, but by far the most use they see are in repairing and modifying things I already have. From fixing the frayed edge of my day pack to  mending those treasured but past-it jeans and saving a caravan holiday when my friend’s awning was found to have a rip, they are as important tools to me as my multimeter or oscilloscope.
  If you’ve made it this far and fancy looking for a sewing machine to grace your own bench, where should you start? Given that this is a product which has been in production for over 150 years, you have a huge array of machines to choose from.
 It’s worth beginning at the end of the market that you might not expect us to tackle first, and look at new machines. A new machine might cost more than a second-hand one, but if you buy wisely you should be able to secure plenty of modern features without breaking the bank. Of course just like any tool it is possible to pay a four or five figure sum at the top end of the market, and if that’s the course you take you’ll get what you pay for in the form of a very high quality machine. But if that describes you then you probably won’t need our advice. We’re more interested in the other end of the market for the purposes of this article.
  If you take a look at any department store website, you’ll find a range of budget sewing machines. At the bottom end of the price range, some of them are astoundingly cheap coming in at $50 or under. These are usually own-brand machines aimed at younger users, a sort of “My first sewing machine” product. It’s probably best to view these machines as having more in common with a toy than your grandmother’s trusty Singer, and avoid them. You can make all sorts of things with one, but do not expect it to be robust, or to last long when used for heavy-duty work.
 Happily on the next step up from the toy machines are the base models from quality manufacturers. These share the metal components and reliability of their more expensive brethren, but lack some of the premium features. They will normally have a range of stitches, but their mechanisms will be entirely mechanical rather than computerised. If you have somewhere above $100 to spare they make an excellent choice for someone seeking a workaday sewing machine.
 In the second-hand market you have that century or more of machines to choose from, and some machines can command eye-watering prices while others are almost worthless. It’s best to start with the more recent machines and move backwards in time to catch the sweet spots.
  Most premium machines from the late 1960s through to the 1990s have all the modern features of the day such as the range of stitches, and were depending on the manufacturer built to a very high standard. They are however quite heavy and extremely dated in their appearance, so can often be found in very good condition in the want-ads for not a huge amount of money. A quick scan while writing this piece finds a range of similar machines including ones from very expensive manufacturers advertised for similar prices to the toy machines mentioned earlier. Exchange rates are in a state of flux at the moment so it’s probably unwise to do a conversion, but £25 to £50 seems to get you a lot of features if you don’t mind a machine that’s sewed a few shoulder pads in its time.
 Older machines come from an unbroken line stretching from the 1850s to the 1960s. They all perform very well the single task of stitching in a straight line, and by the early twentieth century their design had evolved to the point at which they were all fairly similar mechanically. The earlier models made before about the First World War are probably best considered for our purposes here as museum pieces, it is the later ones from the second quarter of the century onwards that should interest us.
 Earlier machines like the one in the black-and-white picture at the top of the page were made to be objects of beauty in their own right. They were richly decorated, and there are collectors who buy them for this decoration. As with any field in which collectors move in their prices can thus climb to the point at which a buyer looking only for a usable sewing machine is priced out of the market for them.
  The good news for us comes from mid-century fashion. In the 1950s the decorated machines with their curved castings were seen as ugly and outdated, and customers demanded clean lines with modern hues. Thus the manufacturers took the machines they had been making for decades and reclad them in more angular bodies painted with contemporary colours. Fashion is a funny thing, because these dull brown and green sewing machines are now unwanted and unloved, despite being mechanically identical to their curved and decorated predecessors. This is good news because it means their worth is much less, and persistence may well net you one for something close to beer money.
 These 1950s and early 1960s machines are built to an extremely high standard, and  will sew anything you can throw at them. They’ll probably outlast almost everything else you own. You do need to summon your inner weightlifter to heft one, and they don’t lend themselves to easy shipping. If you can do without any fancy stitches they represent something of a sweet spot, and we’d recommend them as a good place to start if you can get your hands on one.
 Have you changed your mind about textile work reading this article, and are you about to scour your parents loft for your grandmother’s forgotten sewing machine? On a hardware hacker’s bench a sewing machine is just another machine tool, and it’s probably one you can’t afford to be without.
title: 为什么你应该拥有缝纫机（2016年）

我已经有一个巢烟雾报警约7年。 它连接到我的WiFi网络，偶尔会向我的手机挂上一条消息，我烧焦了我的烤面包。 n  WTAF？ 肯定是市场资本主义和摩尔定律的组合意味着这些设备应该大幅下降？ 但我找不到英国出售的任何其他Wi-Fi烟雾/ CO报警。 这是为什么？  （有90英镑的Netamo Wifi烟雾探测器 - 但它不做一氧化碳。）  一个基本的烟雾＆amp; 一氧化碳探测器约为20英镑的零售价。 该价格点有数百种型号。  一个基本的ESP32 WiFi板是纤维。 如果您想要像覆盆子Pi Zero零W.的东西，请称为Tenner  添加了运行一些服务器的成本，您几乎没有刮50英镑。 当然有人在那里想要出售一百万这些东西吗？  但是，我想我可以理解为什么不加载这些待售： 
低市场欲望。虽然不完全是互联网冰箱领域 - 对大多数被动的物联网设备没有巨大的愿望。像洪水传感器和敞篷窗口探测器一样的东西似乎不感兴趣的人。
 合规和保险。我假设这些事情的认证成本很高。如果有人的房子烧毁，但探测器没有警告他们，然后保险公司开始战斗。
 软件很难。巢有一个陷入困境的历史。应用程序需要不断更新。这意味着设备的高成本，或将人们绑定到订阅计划中。
 专利。我相信谷歌有一堆这些，它可能吓到大多数竞争对手。
 Wi-Fi复杂性。 Wi-Fi是饥饿的力量 - 这使得难以在内置的电池上运行。为用户配置难以配置应用程序，并在无线密码更改时必须重新配置设备。
  ZigBee是IOT的低功耗，低成本，短程无线电网络。发现很多灯泡和其他东西。缺点是它需要在家中的中心集线器，以便连接到网络。从理论上讲，任何集线器都应该连接到任何ZigBee设备 - 我使用带有Hue Hub的一堆通用电灯泡。
 但现实与理论略有不同。在英国，我找不到任何Zigbee烟雾/ CO探测器。有一堆宣称工作，可直接发货。但他们很少有必要的安全认证，他们似乎需要自己的专有集线器。 
栖息地电池是一种适合放置在烟雾报警中的WiFi的电池。 当它检测到指示警报听起来的功率绘制时，它将连接到WiFi。 但是，再一次，它没有再销售。  我需要知道我的房子在我离开锁定时燃烧了吗？ 不，可能不是。 但很有趣！ 我知道隐私风险吗？ 是的 - 我了解如何减轻它们。 我可以用PI和一些清除的组件建立自己吗？ 当然 - 但我不能被打扰。  所以，除非你们其中一个聪明的地段可以推荐聪明的烟雾＆amp; 在英国提供的Carbon Mono探测器 - 它看起来就像我为巢付费谷歌税。 呸!
note_en:
I’ve had a Nest smoke alarm for about 7 years. It connects to my WiFi network and occasionally pings a message to my phone that I’ve burnt my toast. Nifty!
     WTAF? Surely a combination of market capitalism and Moore’s Law means these devices should have dropped in price significantly? But I can’t find any other Wi-Fi smoke/co alarms for sale in the UK. Why is that?
 (There’s a  Netamo WiFi smoke detector for £90 – but it doesn’t do carbon monoxide.)
 A basic smoke & carbon monoxide detector is about £20 retail. There are hundreds of models at that price point.
 A basic ESP32 WiFi board is a fiver. Call it a tenner if you want something like a Raspberry Pi Zero W.
 Add in the cost of running some servers, and you’re barely scraping £50. Surely there’s someone out there who wants to sell a million of these things?
 But, I think I can understand why there aren’t loads of these for sale:
 Low market desire. While not exactly Internet Fridge territory – there isn’t a huge desire for mostly passive IoT devices. Things like flood sensors and open-window detectors just don’t seem to interest people.
 Compliance and insurance. I assume there’s a high cost of certification for these things. And if someone’s house burns down, but the detector didn’t warn them, then insurance companies start to fight.
 Software is hard.  Nest had a troubled history. Apps need constant updating. Which either means a high cost for the device, or tying people into a subscription plan.
 Patents. I’m sure Google has a bunch of these and it probably scares off most competitors.
 Wi-Fi complexity. Wi-Fi is power hungry – which makes it hard to run on in-built batteries. It is also difficult to configure for the user – lots of faffing around with apps and having to reconfigure the device when your wireless password changes.
  ZigBee is the low power, low cost, short range radio network for IoT. Found in lots of lightbulbs and other things. The downside is that it needs a central hub in your home in order to connect to the network. In theory, any hub should connect to any ZigBee devices – I use a bunch of  generic lightbulbs with my Hue hub.
 But the reality is slightly different from the theory. I can’t find any ZigBee smoke/co detectors for sale in the UK. There are a bunch which claim to work, which can be shipped direct from China. But they rarely have the requisite safety certification, and they all seem to need their own proprietary hub.
 The  Roost Battery is a WiFi enabled battery suitable for placing in smoke alarms. When it detects a power draw that indicates the alarm is sounding, it connects to WiFi. But, again, it isn’t sold in the UK any more.
  Do I  need to be alerted that my house is burning down when I leave lockdown? No, probably not. But it’s fun! Am I aware of the privacy risks? Yes – and I understand how to mitigate them. Could I build my own out of a Pi and some scavenged components? Sure – but I can’t be bothered.
 So, unless one of you clever lot can recommend a smart smoke & carbon mono detector which is available in the UK – it looks like I’m paying the Google Tax for the Nest. Bah!
title: 无论IOT烟雾报警发生了什么？
title_en: Whatever happened to IoT smoke alarms?

Hi! Another month of development has passed, and I’m here to fill you in onwhat’s new. Another 686 signups this month has brought us to 21,041 users. Asalways, I’ll be counting on you to make the new users feel at home, please bepatient with them and help them learn the ropes.
 Last month, something suddenly came up and I had to skip the monthly Mumble callunannounced — my apologies! We’ll be holding the meeting as scheduledtomorrow at 16:00 UTC at voice.mnus.de, port 64738, in the sourcehut room.
  The big news this month is that we shipped  sourcehut pages, which providesstatic hosting for websites and Gemini capsules. 282 users have created a sitewith it so far — you should check it out, too! Getting a website publishedonly takes a couple of minutes.
  todo.sr.ht’s initial GraphQL API is now available. Check out the playground here and our  GraphQLdocs if you’re not already familiar with how itworks.
   The GraphQL API for builds.sr.ht is now underway. This one is quite welcome, asbuilds.sr.ht is our oldest sr.ht API and the most in need of an upgrade. It willalso give us some opportunities to improve security for builds.
  A minor update for the project hub this month: popular tags are now shown on the public index.

忙于做不到任何东西是北太平洋日志的修订和改进的电子书版本。这是217页，用英文编写，并格式化为大多数电子阅读器（EPUB，MOBI和PDF）。电子书有51个新图纸，有16个关于各种主题和19个食谱的新部分。
  在2020年，我们完成了太平洋的环保。来自日本（Shimoda）到加拿大（维多利亚州）的最后一篇文章花了51天，这是我们曾经做过的最难的事情。我们决定在板上保持一日事业的物理日志。
   在2020年代，我们决定扩展它，并将其释放为数字图书。我们修改了大部分文本，添加了新艺术并在特定日志中扩展。
 这本书是用Markdown编写的，并通过Pandoc使用乳胶和CSS格式化到PDF和EPUB。第一个繁忙的忙碌没有任何东西没有任何东西在2月17日起发布。这本书是开源的，但购买它是一种支持我们的工作的方式。北太平洋日志仍然在线，但不包括新内容。
note_en:
Busy Doing Nothing is the revised and improved e-book version of the  North Pacific Logbook. It is 217 pages long, written in English, and formatted for most e-readers (EPUB, mobi and PDF). The e-book has 51 new drawings, 16 new sections on a variety of topics and 19 recipes.
  In 2020, we completed our circumnavigation of the Pacific ocean. The last passage from Japan (Shimoda) to Canada (Victoria) took 51 days, and it was the hardest thing we've ever done. We decided to keep a physical logbook of daily happenings onboard.
   In fall 2020, we decided to expand on it, and to release it as a digital book. We revised most of the text, added new art and expanded on specific logs.
 The book was written in Markdown and formatted to PDF and EPUB using  LaTeX and CSS via  Pandoc. The first edition of Busy Doing Nothing was released on February 17th 2021. The book is  open source, but buying it is a way of supporting our work. The  North Pacific Logbook is still online, but does not include new content.
title: 忙着做什么

note:
2003年，Praveen Kumar购买了他的第一部手机，因为他的几个朋友已经拥有了。但是对于来自Bihar的东方29岁的教师，印度最贫困的一位，使用该设备与他的朋友交谈是不可行的。 “这是非常昂贵的，”Kumar说。每个外向一分钟都被收取约8美分，因此10分钟的呼叫成本约为当时的平均日常工资。 “我们没有足够的钱来说手机上的一切。”
 因此，Kumar的朋友和家人开始互相响，但在被收取电话之前挂机;由此产生的未错过呼叫警报函数作为它们之间的代码。 “这是提前决定的，”Kumar说。 “我们会说，如果我来接你，我会给你一个错过的电话，你走出你的房子。”
 以这种方式留下未接听的呼叫 - 有效地使用手机作为一种后一天的寻呼机 - 是一个消费者黑客，在2000年代，在印度的廉价智能手机和数据革命之前，比发短信更受欢迎。在印度出现的未接呼吁作为为那些计算在充值信贷上的每个卢比的人的批判性手段。但是这种做法很快传播，变得时髦，而且，即使在2000年代到世界上最低的呼叫利率，也进化成一般的便利工具：错过的呼叫可能意味着“我想念你”，“”我想你“ ，“或”我在这里。“未错过的呼叫要求只有基本的数字素养使他们能够访问印度文盲的三分之一。 2008年，一项研究估计，超过一半的印度手机用户习惯于呼吁人们期望他们不会接受。
 然后，正如错过的呼叫变得无处不在那样 - 印度的时代在2009年写道，印度人的标志“迅速挂起” - 班加罗尔的一家名为Zipdial的公司拿走了工具并改变了它。在适当的ZiPDial热线中，客户接收了自动化文本和回调，为大型比赛提供了现场板球成绩，这是一笔经济实惠的洗发水，为宝莱坞歌曲的初始需求广播，或名人推文 - 所提供的正在努力接触离线消费者的品牌。在Exchange，公司了解到他们的客户的偏好，并为其产品创建了病毒的离线营销活动。
 在印度的十分之一的人口中在线的时候 - 智能手机非常昂贵，并且购买了一个千兆字节的移动数据，这是主要城市以外的剧烈和痛苦的速度，普通农村印度两到三天的工资 - 未接来电从一个拨号的其他无人数字世界中的呼叫。用户只需要一个功能手机：那种带有号码的垫，预装了一场蛇游戏。 “对于许多人来说，徒普利是与互联网的第一个联系，”Sanjay Swamy是公司的创始人之一。
 随着过去五年的技术变革的疯狂步伐，印度的互联网支付墙在很大程度上落下。预算智能手机和污垢便宜的数据速率确保了一半的印第安人在线。错过的呼叫只是关于过时的，他们的功能更好地由Whatsapp，YouTube，Facebook，Twitter和电子商务应用程序的焊机服务。
 Zipdial最终迎接错过的呼叫自己。该公司的创始人从一开始就预计，他们的工具的有用性将被印度的快速技术进步突出。但2010年之间，当它启动，2016年，它停止运营时，它设法累积了6000万用户，并且在其峰值时，每天提供超过500万个电话。 Zipdial  - 和错过的呼叫的故事 - 不仅展示了印度的变化速度，但企业如何试图在当今客户的需求中展望明天的必然技术进步。 
重新展示错过的商业收益呼吁来到Swamy，企业家和风险资本家，并于2009年的同事瓦莱尼·瓦格纳，在德里到班加罗尔的深夜航班期间。一辆雄心勃勃的Ex-eBay员工瓦格纳曾搬到了来自加利福尼亚州的班加罗尔去年，在MCHEK工作，这是一个由Swamy领导的移动支付初创公司。在航班上，这两者正在讨论印度监测消费者行为的难度，其中超过95％的购买是离线和现金的，当他们意识到他们忽视了一种方法。许多印度客户已经习惯于发短信给广告号码的“短代码”，以获得品牌的优惠和信息。 “我震惊了我可以通过错过的电话做完全相同的事情，”斯沃思说。
 这两个人很快涉及Amiya Tavak，这是一个成为Zipdial第三个联合创始人的软件工程师。在一周之内，Rathak建立了一个系统，用于将呼叫者链接到离线世界中的呼叫者到连接到互联网的后端。它被告知简单：客户会调用热线，手机响两次并自动断开连接，系统将在几秒钟内回复动态内容，例如匹配分数，通过呼叫或文本。
 “错过呼叫行为的美丽是它已经普遍存在了，”瓦格纳说。它已经变得如此常见的是沃克这个国家的电信运营商：未接来电堵塞超过30％的电话线，但没有收入。
 最令人鼓舞的Zipdial的商业前景是其潜在的用户群正在迅速扩大。印度是第一个手机繁荣的中间。根据2006年至2011年，从2006年到2011年，根据世界银行的三分之三的三分之二的人口超过三分之二的人口，从2006年到2011年，移动订阅的数量从1.66亿增加到近8.94亿。 “人们会在拥有银行账户之前有手机，”瓦格纳说。
  作为该工具的测试运行，三个联合创始人在2010年在南非的2010年FIFA世界杯期间建立了一项服务，以便在响应未接呼叫时提供更新的匹配分数。它顺利工作，如果不显眼，直到一天，团队忘了更新分数，并重复用户用投诉淹没它们。 “我们有点像这样做，直到那样，”斯沃思们召回。 “我们说，我的善良，人们实际上取决于这项服务！”
 在2011年ICC蟋蟀世界杯期间，世界上最大的蟋蟀锦标赛之一，Zipdial闯入了大众市场。与上一年一样，Swamy，Wagoner和Pathak设置了一个服务，以更新匹配分数的呼叫者。 “它刚刚起飞就像疯了一样，”瓦格纳说。在第一场比赛期间，ZiPDial在一天内最终加工超过400万个电话。
 Zipdial很快自己的自身来进行铅生成的营销活动，其中客户将拨打错过的呼叫热线在广告牌或电视广告上与产品展开。 “最终，在Zipdial，我们试图解决离线到在线连接，”瓦格纳说，“能够将客户联系到他们关心的东西，是内容或产品。”该模型在2011年取得了成功，Zipdial的第一个大客户，它跟踪了超过10个城市的100万自由剃须样品的受助人。在接下来的两年里，互联网批评了400多名客户，包括像百事可乐，宝贵公司这样的财富500强公司;赌博和迪士尼。 
Zipdial的竞选活动当时很容易表现出社交媒体。到2013年，Gillette通过ZiPDial累积了240万用户，超过其163万个Facebook喜欢。迪士尼使用该平台达到200万用户，超过90％的人没有Facebook帐户或移动互联网;令人难以置信的是，这些用户平均每月从事大约13次，这是一个在发达的市场上缺乏前所未有的数字。
 错过的电话迅速取代了短信，作为印度大品牌选择的营销工具。注意Zipdial的成功，许多电信公司，如vivaconnect和ozonetel，从2010年开始，为自己的reptoire添加了未错过的呼叫活动。2010年中期和2013年初，Zipdial的收入占据了六倍，印度的经济时期估计未错过的呼叫行业总额为50亿卢比 - 约为9400万美元。
 错过的呼吁被证明是一个惊人的多功能工具。 ZiPDial在2012年和2015年提出的专利设想了一系列场景，其中一些方案，其中一些方案，例如未接来电呼叫在两个呼叫者之间交换名片或交换名片，将在很大程度上是有抱负的。其他人，例如错过的对现实展示参赛者投票的呼吁，是通过私营和竞争对手的竞争对手实现的。
   2013年，作为Zipdial的客户之一的联合利华与其对手ozonetel合作，为媒介建立了创新使用：基于移动的按需音乐服务，称为Kan Khajura Tesan，“耳虫站”。要收听坎卡村，比哈拉郡的居民和贾克克手 - 印度国家的日常发货量很常见，但80％的家庭现在拥有至少一个手机套装 - 将发出错过的电话。作为回报，他们将收到15分钟的鲍尔佩伍德内容，主要是电影的百分之一和歌曲，穿插着联合利华广告。 “当时，你无法轻易听歌曲，”一名23岁的学生和普通的Kan Khajura订阅者说，Prashant Harsh说。 “你必须付钱让他们从存储卡中的商店。”
 Kan Khajura的中央呼吁是，与广播电视不同，它可以随时随地访问。此外，流自由，材料总是新鲜的。该站还将根据呼叫者的互动来调整。如果客户过早地结束了呼叫，则算法会将播放列表加倍，以便随后的会话中播放播放列表，使得站在地面无线电和基于互联网的基于互联网的服务之间运行的类型，例如Spotify或YouTube之间的一种交叉。 Kan Khajura长大于比哈尔尔最多的“无线电频道”成为，全国各地约有5000万用户。
 未接呼叫，2013年开始弥合离线用户与社交媒体的扩大世界之间的差距。 Zipdial与Twitter合作，让用户“订阅”对名人的饲料 - 以Shah Rukh Khan，Bollywood Superstar  - 通过未接来电开始，推文作为用户手机交付的推文。它与Facebook合作，嵌入了其平台上的错过呼叫按钮，因此印度移动用户可以离线与广告品牌互动，而不会产生额外的数据费用。
 然而，Zipdial，行业的海报孩子，总是坚持认为这是“不仅仅是一个错过的公司”。当然，要称之为，这将是短暂的愿景。正如瓦格纳在2013年在2013年在2013年讲述一份报纸那样“谷歌分析为离线世界的谷歌分析”，创始人们希望该公司定位。 ZiPDial旨在“在他们与互联网连接之前很久了解最多的客户” - 在客户所做的情况下继续有用的信息。该公司处理大数据，并在脱机印度，未接来电是收集它的理想工具。 
瓦格纳说，创始人预测在线社交媒体将最终赢得胜利。 “但是有一个很好的五个或七年窗口，人们尚未将互联网与他们可以联系到这些平台的规模。”
    但印度有多迅速上网？ “我有一个朋友将Zipdial的模型描述为阳光下的冰块：许多用例最终会消失，”Swamy说。
 早在2014年，很明显，Swamy的冰块已经开始融化。基于Chennai的Matchaking App的所有者Anil Kumar回忆说，当他首先注意到他的办公室里的年轻专业人士通过他们的新智能手机轻弹时，他非常感到惊讶。 “我想，”哇，我付出足够的支付给你买得起智能手机吗？“”这些设备是他们每月工资的大量比例和一个标志，他说，智能手机将被视为“经济实惠的奢侈品”。
 随着移动采用率加速，智能手机持续增殖，特别是作为Micromax等印度和中国制造商，如Micromax和Xiaomi进入市场，销售设备只需80美元。 2013年，印度总智能手机用户数量膨胀至1.17亿，比上年增加了55％。
  “我们观察并预测了这些变化，并建立了私有平台的发展方式，”瓦格纳说。因此，视频链接与回调和WhatsApp消息一起发送，补充了SMSES。 2014年，Zipdial最突出的运动是在线缓解新的智能手机所有者。亚马逊，弗利卡特和超级公司如竞选活动，发送给呼叫者链接到他们的官方应用程序。 “今天，人们会说，去App Store或播放商店并下载我的应用程序，”Swamy说。但是当时，“发现是更简单的，给他们一个电话。”
 晚些时候，那一年，瓦格纳，路径和斯瓦米斯考虑到“消费者连通性的下一阶段”。 ZiPDial希望在品牌和其6000万客户群之间继续接口，也许通过应用程序。但是，他们收到了梦想优惠：Twitter想购买ZiPDial，以便报告30-40万美元，在科技公司在印度的第一次收购。
 Twitter在一份关于Zipdial技术将有助于“对每个人更易于获得的巨大内容”的陈述。为ZiPDial提供了感觉：它也可以在Twitter的金钱和销售团队的帮助下将未接听的呼叫推向新市场，尽管在单一客户的服务中。 
Zipdial的创始人是欣喜若狂的。 “我们当时为印度创业生态系统完成了一些非凡的东西，”瓦格纳说。 “Zipdial是印度技术创新的那个时间的闪亮例子。”
   在明年，Twitter扩展了通过未接来电可用的内容数量，将更多的印度名人和政治家加入其名单。然而，在2016年底，销售后一年多一半，它会改变课程，以重点关注其核心产品。其中几家子公司突然关闭，包括齐普利。 “我所做的最后一件事之一就是摆脱了所有的Zipdial人，”Wagoner说，然后是Twitter的增长高级总监。 “任何非核心是杀人，杀了。卖掉，关闭。“
 此举有效地终止了Zipdial的操作。但到2016年，在印度的Tech  - 由于其在线人口扩大的扭曲速度而变化，鉴于稳步下降的数据速率 - 被设定为经历转型。 “我不会在2016年再次建造同一家公司，即我建于2009年，”瓦格纳说。该公司的创始人无法预测印度如何移动过去的呼叫。
 2015年，一年进入纳伦德拉·莫伊的总理统治，政府的“数字印度”活动发誓要增加全国各地的互联网连接。 2016年底，Mukesh Ambani，Modi和Now India India最富有的人，正式推出他的新电信公司，Reliance Jio，很有希望能够扩展到所有印第安人的便宜4G互联网。
 JIO的推出价格如此荒谬 - 新客户每天有四个免费的数据 - 即在后周的时间，长的队列在出口之外蜿蜒而落在其外。 JIO SIM卡在黑市上销售。它的竞争对手恐慌并削减了自己的税率，保持竞争力。在随后的价格战的结束时，2015年的大约226卢比的一个GB数据的成本从大约226卢比下降到2016年的76卢比，到19卢比，它仍然向下培训。
 凭借JIO，移动数据的年龄比预期的价格远远快得多，“瓦格纳说。 Ambani带来了Zipdial在线定位的人。管道母公司的深口袋，JIO增加了其电话塔的数量，以便在竞争对手网络易于失败的农村地区提供可靠的数据覆盖。到2017年底，它已达到1.3亿用户。
 因此，印度的数据使用爆炸了。 2015年，平均印度用户每月消耗约805 MB的数据;到2017年，这一数字已经上升了七倍，达到5.7 GB。 Vikram Raichura，该公司的创始人兼首席执行官，vivaconnect等移动营销公司在千禧年的千禧一代，千禧一代的近期呼叫参与下降。正如数百万印度人加入了像Facebook这样的社交媒体平台，当他们可以在网上更便宜地驾驶流量时，品牌就不愿意支付错过的呼叫活动。 
Kan Khajura Historer Harsh从铁路站和政府办公室潜伏在恐怖站，以便在他的指尖中拥有迅速的互联网。响起的日子kan khajura很快就会蒸发到过去。 “你可以以最快的方式让一切都能得到”“他说。 “使用所有其他服务都没有意义。”与数百万其他印度人一样，他能够从任何地方观看YouTube视频，而无需担心对他的数据使用率进行配给。花了几年依靠错过的电话来接收板球更新，“JIO的发射后改变了，”他说。 “现在，即使你在外面，你也可以看蟋蟀比赛，而不是错过一个球。”
    Zipdial的一大堆成功流出了它在运行期间能够适应印度改变技术景观的事实。 “作为创始人，你必须在今天的客户需求之间找到战略平衡，但同时为您了解您将来需要创新的基础，”瓦格纳说。 “我们随时准备创新并回应市场动态。”所以错过的呼吁开始作为互联网的替代品，然后成为它的桥梁，因为印度人在线移动，直到JIO的深刻破坏性造成了不必要的角色。
 在回想起来，瓦格纳说，ZiPDial是一种关闭消费者和印度在线服务和产品之间存在的“离线在线差距”的方法。 Wagoner表示，这是一个仍然存在的差距，但随着需求的不断变化，正在以不同的方式解决：通过数字支付系统。
 剩下的未错过呼叫模型的一些例子。银行仍然提供未错过的呼叫服务，例如作为获取帐户陈述的方式。他们在政治中很受欢迎，特别是与印度的裁决党的Bharatiya Janata党。 2020年代初，BJP推出了一个错过的呼叫运动，以鼓励对一个有争议的法案，“公民修正法”，该法案试图将来自穆斯林 - 多数国家的穆斯林难民排除在成为印度国民。该党声称它收到了超过500万个支持票据的支持。
 但如果在一个角度看到错过的呼叫，就像方便和平等主义一样 - 他们帮助在2011年推出了一个大规模的反腐败运动 - 因为他们已经远离主流，只有在最贫穷的家庭中成为一个夹具，他们使用的光学器件已经改变。 Keyoor Purani是印度管理学会Kozhikode学院营销教授Keyoor Purani表示，今天被认为是一种将政治家联系起来的方式。
 这一切却确定了错过呼叫的任何剩余应用程序很快就会变得多余。到2022年，预计印度为8.2亿智能手机，而小学队认为它是kumar的广泛问题，是印第安人在所有时间都被粘在一起，被其产品所吸引。 2018年，印度普通的每月数据使用量从4.13 GB达到每月4.69 GB，是世界上最高的。 “错过的呼叫日子不那么忙碌，”他说

In 2003, Praveen Kumar bought his first mobile phone, as several of his friends already had. But for the 29-year-old schoolteacher from the eastern state of Bihar, one of India’s poorest, to use the device to talk to his friends was inadvisable. “It was very costly,” Kumar says. Each outgoing minute was charged about eight cents, so a 10-minute call cost about as much as the average daily wage at the time. “We didn’t have enough money to say everything on the phone.”
 So Kumar’s friends and family members began ringing each other but hanging up before being charged for a call; the resulting missed-call alerts functioned as a kind of code between them. “It was decided in advance,” Kumar says. “We would say, if I’m coming to pick you up, I’ll give you a missed call, and you come out of your house.”
 Leaving missed calls in this way — effectively using a mobile phone as a kind of latter-day pager — was a consumer hack that, in the 2000s, before India’s cheap smartphone and data revolution, grew more popular than texting. The missed call emerged in India as  a critical means of communication for those who counted every rupee spent on recharge credit. But the practice soon spread,  became trendy, and, even as call rates plunged in the 2000s to among the lowest in the world, evolved into a general tool of convenience: a missed call could mean “I miss you,” “Call me back,” or “I’m here.” The fact that the missed call demanded only basic numeric literacy made them accessible to the third of India’s population that was illiterate. In 2008,  one study estimated that more than half of Indian phone users were in the habit of calling people with the expectation that they wouldn’t pick up.
 Then, just as the missed call became ubiquitous —  The Times of India  wrote in 2009 of Indians’ marked fondness “for hanging up swiftly” — a company in Bangalore called ZipDial took the tool and transformed it. With a couple of rings to the appropriate ZipDial hotline, customers received automated texts and callbacks that delivered live cricket scores for a big match, a deal on an affordable shampoo, rudimentary on-demand radio for Bollywood songs, or celebrity tweets — content supplied by brands that were struggling to reach offline consumers. In exchange, companies learned about their customers’ preferences and created viral offline marketing campaigns for their products.
 At a time when less than a tenth of India’s population was online — smartphones were prohibitively expensive, and buying a gigabyte of mobile data, which was glitchy and agonizingly slow outside of major cities, cost the average rural Indian two to three days’ wages — missed calls made information from an otherwise unreachable digital world available with a single dial. Users needed only a feature phone: the kind with a number pad, preloaded with a game of Snake. “For many people, ZipDial was the first connection to the internet,” says Sanjay Swamy, one of the company’s founders.
 With the frantic pace of technological change over the past five years, the internet pay wall in India has largely come down. Budget smartphones and dirt-cheap data rates have ensured that half of Indians are online. And missed calls are just about obsolete, their function better served by WhatsApp, YouTube, Facebook, Twitter, and a welter of e-commerce apps.
 ZipDial eventually went the way of the missed calls themselves. The company’s founders anticipated from the beginning that the usefulness of their tool would be outpaced by rapid tech advancements in India. But between 2010, when it launched, and 2016, when it ceased operations, it managed to accumulate 60 million unique users and, at its peak, was servicing over 5 million calls a day. The story of ZipDial — and missed calls — demonstrates not only the speed of change in India but how businesses can try to balance looking after the needs of today’s customers with the inevitable technological advances of tomorrow.
    The idea of repurposing missed calls for commercial gain came to Swamy, an entrepreneur and venture capitalist, and his colleague Valerie Wagoner in 2009, during a late-night flight from Delhi to Bangalore. Wagoner, an ambitious ex-eBay employee, had moved to Bangalore from California the previous year to work at mChek, a mobile payments startup headed by Swamy. On the flight, the two were discussing the difficulty of monitoring consumer behavior in India, where over 95% of purchases were made offline and in cash, when they realized there was one method they had overlooked. Many Indian customers were already in the habit of texting “short codes” to advertised numbers to receive offers and information from brands. “It struck me that you could do the exact same thing through a missed call,” Swamy said.
 The two soon involved Amiya Pathak, a software engineer who would become ZipDial’s third co-founder. Within a week, Pathak had built a system for linking callers in the offline world to a back end connected to the internet. It was beguilingly simple: a customer would call a hotline, the phone would ring twice and automatically disconnect, and the system would reply within seconds with dynamic content, such as match scores, through a call or texts.
 “The beauty of the missed-call behavior was that it was already pervasive,” Wagoner said. It had become so commonplace as to vex the country’s telecom operators: missed calls  clogged up over 30% of phone lines but generated no revenue.
 Most encouraging for ZipDial’s business prospects was that its potential user base was rapidly expanding. India was in the midst of its first mobile phone boom. In five years, from 2006 to 2011, the number of mobile subscriptions had soared from 166 million to nearly 894 million, according to the World Bank — over two-thirds of the country’s population at the time. “People would have mobile phones before they would have bank accounts,” Wagoner said.
  As a test run for the tool, the three co-founders set up a service during the 2010 FIFA World Cup in South Africa to provide updated match scores over text in response to a missed call. It worked smoothly, if inconspicuously, until, one day, the team forgot to update scores, and repeat users flooded them with complaints. “We were kind of doing it as a hobby until then,” Swamy recalled. “We said, my goodness, people actually depend on this service!”
 During the 2011 ICC Cricket World Cup, one of the world’s biggest cricket tournaments, ZipDial broke into the mass market. As in the previous year, Swamy, Wagoner, and Pathak set up a  service to update callers about match scores. “It just took off like crazy,” Wagoner says. During the first match, ZipDial ended up processing over 4 million calls in a day.
 ZipDial soon fashioned itself to conduct lead-generating marketing campaigns, in which customers would dial the missed-call hotline featured alongside a product on a billboard or TV ad. “Ultimately, at ZipDial, we were trying to solve the offline-to-online connection,” Wagoner says, “to be able to connect customers to the things that they cared about, be that content or products.” That model found success in 2011 with Gillette, ZipDial’s first big customer, with whom it tracked the recipients of 1 million free razor samples in over 10 cities. Over the next two years, ZipDial accrued more than 400 clients, including a parade of Fortune 500 companies like Pepsi, Procter & Gamble, and Disney.
 ZipDial’s campaigns easily outperformed social media at the time. By 2013, Gillette  had accumulated 2.4 million subscribers through ZipDial, surpassing its 1.63 million Facebook likes. Disney used the platform to reach 2 million subscribers, over 90% of whom didn’t have a Facebook account or mobile internet; incredibly, these subscribers engaged about 13 times per month on average,  a figure that would have been unprecedented in developed markets.
 Missed calls swiftly replaced SMS-only campaigns as the marketing tool of choice for big brands in India. Noting ZipDial’s success, a number of telecommunications firms, such as VivaConnect and Ozonetel, added missed-call campaigns to their own repertoire, starting in 2010. Between mid-2012 and early 2013, ZipDial’s revenue shot up sixfold, and India’s  Economic Times  estimated that the missed-call industry was worth 5 billion rupees in total — around $94 million.
 The missed call proved to be an astonishingly versatile tool. Patents filed by ZipDial in 2012 and 2015 envision a range of scenarios for its use, some of which, such as missed calls to switch credit cards on and off or exchange business cards between two callers, would remain largely aspirational. Others, such as  missed calls to vote for reality show contestants, were realized by ZipDial and its competitors across a number of marketing campaigns.
   In 2013, Unilever, which was one of ZipDial’s clients, partnered with its rival Ozonetel to establish an innovative use for the medium: a mobile-based on-demand music service called Kan Khajura Tesan, “the Earworm Station.” To listen to Kan Khajura, residents of Bihar and Jharkhand — Indian states where daily power outages were common, but 80% of households now owned at least one mobile phone set — would send a missed call to the station. In return, they would receive a callback with 15 minutes of Bollywood content, mostly banter and songs from films, interspersed with Unilever ads. “At the time, you couldn’t listen to songs easily,” says Prashant Harsh, a 23-year-old student and regular Kan Khajura subscriber. “You had to pay to get them from a shop in a memory card.”
 Kan Khajura’s central appeal was that it could be accessed anywhere and anytime, unlike the radio and TV. Moreover, streaming was free, and the material was always fresh. The station would also adapt on the basis of a caller’s interactions. If a customer ended a call prematurely, algorithms would shuffle the playlist for the subsequent session, so that the station functioned as a kind of cross between terrestrial radio and curated internet-based services such as Spotify or YouTube. Kan Khajura grew to become, for several years, the most accessed  “radio channel” in Bihar and had around 50 million subscribers across the country.
 The missed call, in 2013, began bridging the gap between offline users and the expanding world of social media. ZipDial partnered with Twitter to allow users to “subscribe” to celebrities’ feeds — beginning with that of Shah Rukh Khan, the Bollywood superstar — through a missed call, with Tweets delivered as texts to users’ phones. It partnered with Facebook, which embedded a missed-call button on its platform so Indian mobile users could interact with advertised brands offline, without incurring extra data charges.
 However, ZipDial, the industry’s poster child, always maintained that it was “not just a missed-calls company.” Certainly, to call it that would be to shortchange its vision. The founders wished to position the company, as Wagoner told  one newspaper in 2013, as “Google Analytics for the offline world.” ZipDial aimed to “know the most about the most number of customers long before they got connected to the internet” — information that would continue to be useful long after customers did. The company dealt in big data, and in an offline India, the missed call was the ideal tool to gather it.
 The founders predicted online social media would win out eventually, Wagoner said. “But there was a good five- or seven-year window where people were not yet going to have the internet on the kind of scale that they could reach those platforms.”
    But how quickly would India get online? “I had a friend who described ZipDial’s model to me as an ice cube in the sun: many of the use cases would eventually go away,” Swamy says.
 As early as 2014, it was apparent that Swamy’s ice cube had begun to melt. Anil Kumar, the owner of a matchmaking app based in Chennai, recalled that he was pleasantly surprised when he first noticed young professionals in his office flicking through their new smartphones. “I thought, ‘Wow, do I pay you enough to afford a smartphone?’” The devices were a sizable fraction of their monthly wage and a sign, he said, that smartphones would come to be seen as an “affordable luxury.”
 As the mobile adoption rate accelerated, smartphones continued to proliferate, especially as Indian and Chinese manufacturers like Micromax and Xiaomi entered the market in force, selling devices for as little as $80. In 2013, the number of total smartphone users in India had  swelled to 117 million, a 55% increase from the previous year.
  “We observed and anticipated these changes and built them into how the ZipDial platform evolved,” Wagoner said. So video links were sent alongside callbacks, and WhatsApp messages supplemented SMSes. In 2014, ZipDial’s most prominent campaigns were directed at easing new smartphone owners online. Companies such as Amazon, Flipkart, and Uber ran campaigns that sent callers links to their official apps. “Today, people will say, Go to the App Store or Play store and download my app,” Swamy said. But at the time, “the discovery was a lot simpler to give them a number to call.”
 Later that year, Wagoner, Pathak, and Swamy were considering a move to the “next phase of consumer connectivity.” ZipDial wanted to continue to interface between brands and its 60 million customer base, perhaps through an app. But then, they received a dream offer: Twitter wanted to purchase ZipDial for  a reported $30–40 million, in what would be the tech company’s first acquisition in India.
 Twitter said  in a statement about the deal that ZipDial’s technology would help “make great content more accessible to everyone.” Taking the offer made sense for ZipDial as well: it could push the missed call into new markets with the help of Twitter’s money and sales team, albeit in the service of a single client.
 ZipDial’s founders were ecstatic. “We had accomplished something extraordinary for the Indian startup ecosystem at that time,” Wagoner said. “ZipDial was a shining example for that time in India’s tech innovation.”
   Over the next year, Twitter expanded the amount of content available via missed calls, adding more Indian celebrities and politicians to its roster. However, in late 2016, just over a year and a half after the sale,  it altered course to focus on its core product. Several of its subsidiaries were abruptly shuttered, including ZipDial. “One of the last things I did was lay off all the ZipDial people,” said Wagoner, who was by then Twitter’s senior director for growth. “Anything non-core was ‘Kill, kill. Sold off, shut down.’”
 The move effectively terminated ZipDial’s operations. But by 2016, tech in India — already changing at warp speed as its online population expanded, given steadily falling data rates — was set to undergo a transformation. “I would not have built that same company again in 2016 that I built in 2009,” Wagoner said. The company’s founders could not have been able to predict just how swiftly India would move past the missed call.
 In 2015, one year into Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s rule, the government’s “Digital India” campaign vowed to increase internet connectivity across the country. In late 2016, Mukesh Ambani, a close ally of Modi and now India’s richest person, formally launched his new telecom company, Reliance Jio, promising to extend cheap 4G internet to all Indians.
 Jio’s prices at launch were so ludicrous — new customers were given four free GB of data per day — that for weeks afterward, long queues snaked outside its outlets. Jio SIM cards were sold on the black market. Its competitors panicked and slashed their own rates to remain competitive. By the end of the ensuing price war, the cost of one GB of data had  dropped from roughly 226 rupees in 2015 and 76 rupees in 2016 to 19 rupees, and it was still trending downward.
 The age of mobile data “came much faster than anticipated, thanks to Jio,” Wagoner says. Ambani brought the very people that ZipDial had been targeting online. Plumbing its parent company’s  deep pockets, Jio increased its number of phone towers in order to provide reliable data coverage in rural areas where its competitors’ networks were apt to fail. By late 2017, it had reached 130 million subscribers.
 Data use in India consequently exploded. In 2015, the average Indian user consumed about 805 MB of data per month; by 2017, that figure had risen sevenfold, to 5.7 GB. Mobile marketing companies like VivaConnect saw a serious drop in missed-call engagement among millennials, says Vikram Raichura, the company’s founder and CEO. As millions of Indians joined social media platforms like Facebook, brands became reluctant to pay for missed-call campaigns when they could drive traffic more cheaply online.
 Harsh, the Kan Khajura listener, went from lurking in railway stations and government offices for free Wi-Fi to possessing rapid internet at his fingertips. The days of ringing up Kan Khajura quickly receded into the past. “You can get everything in the fastest possible way,” he said. “There was no point in using all of these other services.” Like millions of other Indians, he was able to watch YouTube videos from anywhere, without having to worry about rationing his data usage. Having spent years relying on missed calls to receive cricket updates, “things changed after Jio’s launch,” he said. “Now, even when you’re outside, you can watch cricket matches and not miss a single ball.”
    A large part of ZipDial’s success flowed from the fact that it was able, during its run, to adapt nimbly to India’s changing tech landscape. “As a founder, you have to find the strategic balance between what today’s customers need but simultaneously lay the foundations for how you know you will need to innovate in the future,” Wagoner says. “We were always ready to innovate and respond to market dynamics.” So the missed call began as an alternative to the internet and then became a bridge to it, as Indians moved online, until the profoundly disruptive influence of Jio rendered even that role unnecessary.
 In retrospect, Wagoner says, ZipDial was a way to close the “offline-to-online gap” that existed between consumers and online services and products in India. It’s a gap that still exists today, Wagoner said, but, with changing needs, is being addressed in a different way: through digital payment systems.
 There are only a few examples of missed-call models left. Banks still offer missed-call services, for instance, as a way to obtain account statements. And they’re popular in politics, especially with the Bharatiya Janata Party, India’s ruling party. In early 2020, the BJP launched  a missed-call campaign to drum up support for a controversial bill, the Citizenship Amendment Act, which sought to exclude Muslim refugees from Muslim-majority countries from becoming Indian nationals. The party claimed that it received over 5 million calls of support in favor of the bill.
 But if missed calls were seen at one point as convenient and egalitarian — they helped launch a massive anti-corruption campaign in 2011 — as they have moved away from the mainstream to become a fixture only in the very poorest households, the optics surrounding their use has changed. Missed-call campaigns are seen today as a way to link politicians to “information-deprived, gullible voters,” says Keyoor Purani, a marketing professor at the Indian Institute of Management Kozhikode.
 It’s all but certain that any remaining applications of the missed call will soon become redundant. By 2022, there are expected to be  820 million smartphones in India, and a widespread issue, as Kumar, the schoolteacher, sees it, is Indians being glued to them at all times, enthralled by its offerings. In 2018, the average Indian’s monthly data usage vaulted from  4.13 GB to 7.69 GB per month, one of the highest in the world. “The missed-call days were less hectic,” he says
......

I recently updated Pixel Wheels banner image on Google Play. That triggered a review of the game: shortly after the update I received a message telling me Pixel Wheels was "not compliant with Google Play Policies". What nefarious activity does the game engage in? Sneak on users? Mine bitcoins?
     Yes. I confess it. I added a link to my  donation page within the game, depriving Google of some precious money it totally cannot survive without! How dare I?!? I am such a bad person.
 Since there is no point arguing with them, I am going to have to build a Google Play flavor of the game, where this link is replaced with a link to the game page. Hopefully the Great Algorithm will accept that. We'll see.
 Meanwhile you can still get the game from  F-Droid or  itch.io, since they do not have a problem with a link to a donation page.

note:
过去，戈利亚的力量将以肘部测量的高度，头盔黄铜，麦克斯的黄铜，在青铜和矛头部的数千钟声中，称为数百秒的铁。如今，戈利亚公司可以雇用另一个歌洛亚岛，例如律师事务所Kilpatrick Townesend，其中650名律师和19个办事处，北美，欧洲和亚洲。该公司拥有“10个世界上最有价值的品牌中的5个成为克利帕特里克汤安，以发展和捍卫其产品和企业的价值。”其中一个“10个世界上最有价值的品牌中的5个”中的一个对Youtuber Louis Rossmann制造的视频感兴趣。
 当Rossmann由KilPatrick Townsend代表苹果联系时，他觉得如此严峻的收割机在他的门口敲门。一个小企业的所有者，一个苹果设备维修商店，几年后罗斯曼已经开始了一个YouTube频道覆盖了所有对他感兴趣的东西：Apple设备维修，商业咨询，个人建议，偶尔，虽然更加迟到，但社会或政治评论。苹果的注意的视频是Rossmann展示了一个苹果设备的示意图，并开始向观众展示他们如何解决自己的设备，如果他们面临同样的问题。对于Apple，展示YouTube原理图的行为是违反其知识版权。他们希望罗斯曼悄悄地让视频消失。罗斯曼雇用了一名律师，律师建议请求是明智的，没有诉讼，因此合理的事情是遵守;此外，已经对罗斯曼进行了真正的努力 - 这位词来说，这位千克克莱克镇顿和苹果喜欢他的工作。 Rossmann想到了他所获得的选择。然后他解雇了他的律师。
  克帕特里克汤安德的到来给了Rossmann的另一个推动，让更多参与修复运动的权利。修复权是一个全国范围内的努力，旨在使用立法来返回消费者的选择，可以选择它们拥有的设备。 Rossmann认为，现在发生了几十年来的事情即将到来。公司回来尊重消费者的修复他们的东西。原理图是广泛的，您可以在当地电子商店购买它们，或联系制造商将其发送给您。像冰箱一样的电器经常带有一套原理图和如何解决它们的说明。
 Apple试图灌输客户的思想，假设设备仅由公司及其受控网络网络维修。授权维修商店的受控网络用于创造消费者选择的错觉，同时采取加强和巩固公司在修复过程中的垄断。杰萨琼斯是一个独立维修服务的所有者，他们在波士顿作证的一个独立的维修服务，她的小组达到那个点已经固定了30,000个设备，其中“不到5％”，其中“将被授权维修所固定的。”
  罗斯曼不喜欢苹果。他的许多视频都是关于公司如何搞砸，扯掉，折磨，普遍滥用客户。他解释了为什么拥有Apple产品是一个愚蠢的想法，或者宣称最新的操作系统“在球中发出大踢到Apple用户”的视频。罗斯曼有一个真正的，深沉而无情的苹果。
 甚至知道，当我展示罗斯曼的办公室接受采访时，我将一件iPhone放在他旁边，以记录我们的谈话。然后我用我的问题和一个苹果铅笔拔出一个iPad，以击败东西。我抬起手臂，拉起我的袖子，看看一个苹果手表，说“我们准时”。我正在寻找反应，但Rossmann保持冷静，凉爽和收集。在他的脸上有一种轻微的傻笑，这就是我将要得到的一切。
  为了解释他如何在苹果维修中实现苹果机制，同时他强烈地不喜欢公司，它的产品罗斯曼告诉我，“肿瘤学家不喜欢癌症，他们仍然试图帮助患者。”他说，驱动他坚果的是什么，是人们为一个设计缺陷支付的设备，并在Apple的Genius Bar中告诉这个问题是他们使用该设备的方式。 “六个月后，召回计划会出来，他们[顾客]会把它带到[苹果]，并说你能解决这个问题，抱歉不能。人们会一遍又一遍地搞砸了，仍然买它。“ 
还有问题似乎永远不会得到修复。 Rossmann在2016年MAC中谈到了一家四岁的设计缺陷，其中一个“屏幕电源线的52伏特在图像线旁边是一个伏特”为业主创造了严重问题。通常，苹果需要很长时间才能承认这样的问题，有时它永远不会是这样的。在我们采访后的几天后，美国区判断与苹果指出的原告，称“Apple故意销售2016-17 MacBook Pro型号”的灵活性显示缺陷。“
  谈论Apple的滥用是Rossmann的YouTube频道成功的原因之一，现在拥有超过150万用户。如果Apple希望视频下来Rossmann希望他们提出版权声明。在这样做，苹果将不得不公开原因。 “他们不得不说'我们对象展示了融合的地方'，”他说。“我希望你在公开陈述你不希望你的客户知道键盘保险丝在哪里。”
 从2011年开始发现“使用独立汽车维修店的顾客每年在维修时花费大约24％。”这是非常重要的价格差异是实现的，而小型维修店面临实质性的人工障碍。在技​​术领域中，Apple使用其巨大的金融HEFT作为零件的购买者，以迫使其供应商在禁止其销售零件到独立维修店的合同中。 Rossmann必须与全球各地的人们达到Skype，专门从事部分垃圾箱潜水，以便找到供应商不允许出售他的部分。在其他时候，他必须购买一个整个设备，只能从中检索单个芯片。如果允许开放式市场在零件，原理图和图表方面运行，人们只能想象消费者的节省。
 2018年通过CBC展示加拿大公共广播服务，突出了苹果的掠夺性做法。由于CBC希望测试苹果客户“疯狂过度充电”的普遍看法，将MacBook带到Apple Repary进行维修。 Apple Store员工通知卧底记者，修复计算机将花费1200美元。他们可能也可以获得一台新电脑。然后CBC将与纽约罗斯曼同样的电脑带来相同的电脑。
 Rossmann需要几秒钟来弄清楚问题，大约一分钟半来解决它。有“一个伸出的销钉。”放置销钉，连接器插入。解决问题和零充电。该节目继续显示苹果阻碍的许多方式。特殊的非标螺钉，因此设备不易打开，胶合电池不需要粘在一起等。然后有计划过时的问题，在系统更新后，较旧的iPhone会变得显着较慢。所有人都能使独立维修更加困难;所有都可以购买新设备更实用的选择。
 像Apple等公司的大小允许它从其倡导者的政策的后果中屏蔽自己。在填补国外现金时，在家里留下左派政策允许在利润率最佳方面进行德形信号。 Apple在设备维修空间上的小竞争对手没有这些选项。他们不能在国外的廉价血汗工厂工作;他们不能将他们的利润引导到爱尔兰的银行账户。
 苹果公司今天的掠夺性做法成为明天的行业标准。至少达到2019年，Apple在全球手机行业的所有利润中占据了66％。该公司充分利润，因为它为您销售您的新设备销售新设备的途径将每种可能的便士限制和挤出修复过程中的所有可能的便士。如何在同一空间中竞争的任何其他公司比苹果在销售新设备的情况下赚得更少，为其董事会和股东销售可以轻松而廉价地修复的设备？手机业务是一个艰难的，三星获得了17％的利润，而其他人则涉及剩下的东西。 4月5日LG宣布它是退出“令人难以置信的竞争手机行业”。 
Apple使用收购来消除竞争，获取未来的竞争对手或获取技术，可能会提供其目前的竞争对手。 GlobalData是一家信息服务公司，发现苹果公司“于2016年至2020年之间购买了比其他人更多的AI公司。”谈到独立维修店，Apple采用了不同的策略。它试图取消他们作为一个想法，作为业务的一种方式，作为合法的消费者选择。计划过时即将到达独立维修店。有趣的是Apple如何通过CBC回复上述新闻报告。它选择在自己的防守中说的是“他们的客户最好由Apple的经过真实的专家使用真正的零件服务。”显然，苹果公司对其供应商施加的合同限制是使其更好的原因。
 在过去的几年里，修复支持者在美国跨国公司聚集在州立立法机，试图建立一个竞争力的维修市场。一些Rossmann的视频带我们来到这些举措。修复船员的权利似乎不适合他们的牛仔裤，T恤和连帽衫的力量，是一种不合适的会众。我们已经毫无思索地成为普通人试图说服他们的代表的视觉。建筑物中有立法者，企业游说者，然后是这些人。这些人是唯一一个失去金钱的人。随着Rossmann与他的麦克风一起去，我们在不知名的地方遇到了与维修店的家伙;我们遇见杰萨琼斯，留下宾夕法尼亚州妈妈，博士学位。在她的小孩双胞胎后开始固定装置的分子遗传学，在厕所里刷新了她的全新iPhone;我们遇到了早晨凌晨在沃尔玛在沃尔玛搁置的那家伙，刚刚成功。
 在另一边，企业游说侧，一切都是合适的和豪华。右鞋，正确的领带，衬衫和西装。这台机器的骄傲齿轮。在立法机关，他们在家里觉得就有它。他们知道他们可以杀死委员会修理账单的权利。没什么可担心的。他们的陈述是通用的，公式化的，强烈的非特异性，并且到自杀点的乏味。 Rossmann现在正在努力直接向人们带着Gofundme的人们在几天内筹集成千上万的人。然而，修理运动权和独立维修店的未来似乎不确定。
 什么并不不确定是关于：修复的权利只不过是努力恢复个人的所有权权利。这是一个与新的生命中的新订阅模式相反，您总是远离丢失它的人。集中控制的环境，一切都是由管理课程的一切始终监督，策划和监控，对个人持怀疑态度持怀疑态度。我们被调节到数字Serfdom的状态，好像它已经算法决定，个别选择和个性都不是更多。修复的权利是我们面前的故障未来的故障。
